supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content:
This design covers the server-role TLS-seam slice that derives the first TLS 1.3 application packet-protection material after the supported Finished proof boundary and publishes explicit open/protect material through the existing bridge state.
It does not add 1-RTT stream/data processing, key update, endpoint-host wiring, or interop-harness behavior.

purpose: Describe how the server-role 1-RTT packet-protection publication slice satisfies the CRT requirement that follows the supported Finished proof boundary.
risks: If the new directional 1-RTT material is treated like generic 1-RTT readiness, the bridge will overstate data-path support.
If terminal-state cleanup does not clear the directional material, later fatal-alert transitions can leak stale publication state.
If client-role behavior starts honoring the server-only directional updates, the existing client seam will regress.
design_summary:
The managed key schedule derives 1-RTT application traffic secrets from the transcript boundary through local Finished, but the bridge exposes the directional open/protect material only after the server role has proven the peer Finished.
The bridge state stores the new directional 1-RTT material separately from the legacy generic packet-protection map so publication remains explicit without enabling the 1-RTT data path.
The connection runtime does not add a 1-RTT send/receive path in this slice; it simply preserves the new bridge-visible material and leaves key update and endpoint-host work out of scope.
title: QUIC CRT server-role 1-RTT publication architecture
